location.office_time_required: at least 3 days per week, including Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

level_signals: 2–4 years of experience in program management, operations, or a related field
Works with oversight from senior team members
Base pay range CAD $71,200 - CAD $89,000 consistent with associate/junior level

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 2–4 years of experience in program management, operations, or a related field
SQL proficiency (basic to intermediate) — comfortable querying datasets, interpreting dashboards, and doing ad-hoc analysis in tools like Mode
Detail-oriented with strong follow-through; you complete what you start and document what you did
Clear, proactive communicator who keeps stakeholders informed without being prompted
Comfortable reprioritizing in a fast-paced environment based on urgency and business need
Familiarity with project tracking tools (e.g., Jira) and Google Suite
Based in or able to work from Toronto
Willingness to travel up to 25% of the time and have a valid passport. Nights and/or weekends are occasionally required for event support.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Based in or able to work from Toronto; knowledge of local market context a plus

scope.surface: Venue Operations — managing Lyft's presence at thousands of venue locations including sports stadiums, concert venues, conventions, and festivals
primary_metrics_inferred: Venue performance trends and anomaly detection
core_responsibilities: Execute and maintain venue configurations in Scope Studio, including event scheduling, venue pins, road closures, and pickup/drop-off zones
Support new venue launches and configuration updates, ensuring accuracy against physical ground conditions
Participate in venue audits — reviewing existing configurations, identifying gaps, and documenting corrective action
Monitor venue performance using dashboards and analytics tools; surface trends and flag anomalies with context
Manage inbound partner requests through the team's email alias and Jira board, responding within SLA targets
Coordinate with non-contractual partners (NCPs) and internal teams (BD, Legal, Product) to resolve operational issues and fulfill requests
Own execution of assigned workstreams end-to-end, along with special projects
Identify opportunities to improve repetitive processes and document improvements for team use
Provide proactive status updates to stakeholders; escalate blockers with relevant context before they become issues

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned alongside base pay

green_flags: Hybrid schedule clearly defined (3 days/week in office with specific days named)
Role fills an existing vacancy — not a speculative hire
Clear ownership and scope described for an associate-level role
Benefits package is detailed and comprehensive (RRSP match, 18 weeks parental leave, etc.)
Cross-functional exposure to engineering, mapping, data, legal, and BD teams
red_flags: Toronto presence required — eliminates remote candidates outside commuting range
Up to 25% travel required plus occasional nights/weekends for event support — schedule flexibility is a real constraint
Compensation stated in CAD, not USD — role is Canada-based only
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed in the JD
